12 crates | 854 tests | CSR + ArrowFragment + R2 | Critical Path + Full-Stack Shannon + Kaizen
deps: grin, cypher, vineyard, core, memmap2
deps: engine, graph, s3, object, axum, tokio, metrics
Write Path — Dirty Label Delta Snapshot [IMPLEMENTED]
2. Snapshot cron: dirty_labels.drain() -> csr_to_fragment_selective(dirty_set) -> selective R2 PUT (dirty blobs only)
3. is_infra_blob (always) | is_dirty_vertex_blob (dirty only) | is_edge_or_topology_blob (if edges changed)
Shannon Information-Theoretic Analysis — Before vs After
P2: trigger_snapshot() serialized ALL labels, uploaded ALL blobs to R2
O(N) network per snapshot. eta = 0.01% (N=1M, delta=100)
P9: csr_to_fragment() extracted properties for ALL vertex labels
O(V_total x P) CPU per snapshot even when only 1 label was dirty
P4: Cold start full page-in (already mitigated: label-selective + topology-only)
P5: Coordinator 2ms fan-out overhead (mitigated: adaptive sqrt(N))
P6: Dual persistence — Pipeline WAL + R2 snapshot (by design, cold start <5s)
Clean labels = empty table (skip O(V x P))
Shannon Efficiency (N=1M vertices, delta=100 per snapshot)
O(V x P) all labels property scan
O(V_dirty x P) dirty labels only
Delta PUT reduces R2 I/O cost
12 crates | ~22,000 LOC | 854 tests pass | Edition 2024 | Apache-2.0 | cargo zigbuild x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu
Latency breakdown per operation | Bottleneck nodes in red | Production E2E measured values
Write lock blocks all reads during rebuild
Dirty label delta PUT (skip clean labels)
PDS Worker + YataRPC Coordinator + yata Container + XRPC Transport | Redundancy identification + Kaizen
S1: commit() 6-way index rebuild — label_index + label_bitmap + columnar_cache = 3x same data
yata-store/src/lib.rs:2099 | rebuild_label_index O(V*L) + rebuild_label_bitmap O(V*L) + rebuild_columnar_cache O(V*P)
Shannon waste: 200% redundancy. 20M vertices + 10 labels = 200MB bitmap alloc per single-record commit
S2: L1 yata WAL safety net — redundant with L0 Pipeline WAL (exactly-once, durable)
Shannon: L0 Pipeline.send() = source of truth. L1 = safety net while Pipeline Beta. Post-GA: delete L1
S3: ensure_labels() ignores label hints — full page-in despite selective infrastructure existing
Shannon: eta(needed/total) = ~5%. enrich_label_from_r2() exists but not wired to query path
S4: YataRPC regex per-call compilation — FIXED (cached as static fields)
container/yata/src/index.ts:160,256,234 | LABEL_RE, MUTATION_RE, AGG_RE now static readonly + Set dedup
Fix: -50-100us/query. extractLabelHints uses Set instead of Array.includes O(N^2)
S5: Pipeline.send() + mergeRecord() = double JSON serialization of same record
pds-helpers.ts:79 | Array.from(recordBytes) serialized 2x (Pipeline JSON + mergeRecord body)
Shannon: 200-500 bytes/write * 2. Sequential (could parallelize Pipeline + mergeRecord)
CF Container = gVisor sandbox. Workers RPC zero-copy impossible.
HTTP ~1-5ms per hop. Design: CSR <1us absorbs. Not fixable.
index.ts:242 | .catch(warn) only. No retry, no DLQ.
~1us overhead. Could merge timeout into BindingTransport
Needs measurement before tuning. Not Shannon waste.
Incremental rebuild: skip clean labels in rebuild_label_bitmap + rebuild_columnar_cache
Wire label hints to page_in_selective_from_r2 (code exists). Cold start eta 5% to 85%
Cache regex as static readonly. extractLabelHints Set dedup. mergeResults cached AGG_RE
Delete walFlushSegment cron + pushWalToReplicas once Pipeline exits Beta
Parallelize Pipeline.send() + mergeRecord() (independent, no ordering constraint)
Add exponential backoff retry (3x) with DLQ. Reduces silent WAL entry loss
CP2 (Cold) chained to CP3 (Warm): Container wake is one-time. After warm-up, coordinator overhead dominates. Keep-alive heartbeat (5min cron) prevents CP2.
CP5 (Mutation) chained to CP4 (Snapshot): Every mutation dirty flag causes next cron snapshot. At scale, mutation + serialize compete for 1 vCPU.
CP1 (Write) decoupled from CP5 (Mutation): mergeRecord (append, ~500us) bypasses CSR rebuild. Cypher CREATE (~500ms) triggers full rebuild.
S1 (commit rebuild) amplifies CP5: Every mergeRecord calls commit() which does 6 rebuilds. Incremental rebuild (K1) would reduce CP5 from 500ms to ~10ms.
Full-Stack Write Path — Shannon Redundancy Visualization
Full stack: App Worker + PDS Worker + YataRPC Coordinator + yata Container (Write + Read x N) + R2 (Pipeline WAL + Checkpoint) + XRPC Transport
